session.user: Read the file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py and verify the ordering of CommandDefinition entries in get_all_commands(). Extract just the `id` and `description` fields of each CommandDefinition in order. Report them as a simple list so I can verify the alphabetical sorting within each sub-group.
session.assistant: I'll read the file and extract the CommandDefinition entries with their id and description fields.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Command registry for command palette. 2→ 3→This module defines all available commands and their availability predicates. 4→Commands are organized by category: Actions, Opens, Copies. 5→Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view. 6→""" 7→ 8→from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext, CommandDefinition 9→from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode 10→ 11→CATEGORY_EMOJI: dict[CommandCategory, str] = { 12→ CommandCategory.ACTION: "⚡", 13→ CommandCategory.OPEN: "🔗", 14→ CommandCategory.COPY: "📋", 15→} 16→ 17→ 18→# === View Mode Predicates === 19→ 20→ 21→def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: 22→ """True when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn).""" 23→ return ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES 24→ 25→ 26→def _is_objectives_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: 27→ """True when in Objectives view.""" 28→ return ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES 29→ 30→
... [572 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! I've extracted the CommandDefinition entries from the `get_all_commands()` function. Here's the ordered list of id and description fields by category:
## PLAN ACTIONS 1. id: `close_plan` | description: `close` 2. id: `dispatch_to_queue` | description: `dispatch` 3. id: `land_pr` | description: `land` 4. id: `address_remote` | description: `address` 5. id: `rebase_remote` | description: `rebase` 6. id: `rewrite_remote` | description: `rewrite` 7. id: `cmux_checkout` | description: `cmux checkout`
## OBJECTIVE ACTIONS 8. id: `check_objective` | description: `check` 9. id: `close_objective` | description: `close` 10. id: `one_shot_plan` | description: `plan (one-shot)`
## PLAN OPENS 11. id: `open_issue` | description: `plan` 12. id: `open_pr` | description: `pr` 13. id: `open_run` | description: `run`
## OBJECTIVE OPENS 14. id: `open_objective` | description: `objective`
## PLAN COPIES 15. id: `copy_checkout` | description: `checkout` 16. id: `copy_pr_checkout_plain` | description: `checkout` 17. id: `copy_pr_checkout_script` | description: `checkout (cd)` 18. id: `copy_teleport` | description: `teleport` 19. id: `copy_teleport_new_slot` | description: `teleport (new slot)` 20. id: `copy_close_plan` | description: `close` 21. id: `copy_cmux_checkout` | description: `cmux checkout` 22. id: `copy_dispatch` | description: `dispatch` 23. id: `copy_implement_local` | description: `implement` 24. id: `copy_land` | description: `land` 25. id: `copy_replan` | description: `replan` 26. id: `copy_address_remote` | description: `address` 27. id: `copy_rebase_remote` | description: `rebase` 28. id: `copy_rewrite_remote` | description: `rewrite`
## OBJECTIVE COPIES 29. id: `codespace_run_plan` | description: `codespace` 30. id: `copy_plan` | description: `plan` 31. id: `copy_view` | description: `view`
**Summary**: Within each category (by description field within each sub-group), the commands are generally NOT in alphabetical order. For example, in PLAN ACTIONS, you have: close, dispatch, land, address, rebase, rewrite, cmux checkout - which is not alphabetical. Similarly in PLAN COPIES, the descriptions are: checkout, checkout, checkout (cd), teleport, teleport (new slot), close, cmux checkout, dispatch, implement, land, replan, address, rebase, rewrite - also not sorted.
